revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瓢箪山稲荷神社]]
'''Hyoutan-yama Inari Shrine''' (瓢箪山稲荷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Higashiōsaka|en|Higashiōsaka|qid=Q243863|lt=Higashiosaka|1=Higashiōsaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Inari Shrines of Japan|ja|日本三稲荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Inari Shrines of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q719665}} (with varying theories) and is known as the main shrine for {{ill|Tsujiura senbei|en|Tsujiura senbei|qid=Q7850031|lt=Tsuji-uranai|1=Tsujiura senbei}}.
== History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tensho 11|ja|天正11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tensho 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579118|1=1583}} (1583). According to tradition, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|en|Osaka Castle|qid=Q321242|lt=Osaka Castle|1=Osaka Castle}}, enshrined the deity "Fukube Inari" from {{ill|Fushimi Castle|en|Fushimi Castle|qid=Q1196273|lt=Fushimi Castle|1=Fushimi Castle}} as a guardian deity in the southeastern direction of Osaka Castle. The current main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|Keio 2|ja|慶応2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keio 2|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1866年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1866年|1=1866|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7712}} (1866).
Behind the main hall is a small hill known as the "{{ill|Hyoutan-yama Kofun|ja|瓢箪山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoutan-yama Kofun|1=Hyōtan-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11574174}}", a twin circular tumulus dating back to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, around the end of the 6th century. It is the largest and oldest of the {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Kofun Group|qq=}}. The name "Hyoutan-yama" originates from the gourd-like shape of the tumulus.
The shrine has been managed by the Yamahata family for generations.
=== Tsuji-uranai === Since the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, a custom of fortune-telling called {{ill|Tsujiura senbei|en|Tsujiura senbei|qid=Q7850031|lt=Tsuji-uranai|1=Tsujiura senbei}} has been practiced near the {{ill|Higashi Koya Kaido|ja|東高野街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Koya Kaido|1=Higashi Kōya Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11528777}}. In the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine priest formally established Tsuji-uranai, which became widely known throughout Japan with the phrase "Awaji Island’s plover crossing to Kawachi Hyoutan-yama, fortune-telling of love."
== Enshrined Deities == * Principal deity - {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|qid=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}}
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in {{ill|Keio 2|ja|慶応2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keio 2|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1866年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1866年|1=1866|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7712}} (1866). * Worship Hall * Hanakusa Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. Also known as Gozu Tennou Shrine, it serves as the guardian deity. * Togawa Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukemochi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 保食神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 保食神|1=Ukemochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2280323}}, located on the mound behind the main hall. * Mihashira Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. Located north of the worship hall. * Miyahi Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇受売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノウズメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノウズメ}} * Sugawara Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}} * Mizuwake Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Mizuwake-no-Okami|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuwake-no-Okami|qq=}} * Shrine Office * {{ill|Hyoutan-yama Kofun|ja|瓢箪山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoutan-yama Kofun|1=Hyōtan-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11574174}} - A twin circular tumulus from the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. The largest and oldest within the {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Kofun Group|qq=}}.
== Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Station (Osaka)|en|Hyōtan-yama Station (Osaka)|qid=Q11574177|lt=Hyoutan-yama Station (Osaka)|1=Hyōtan-yama Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|Nara Line (Kintetsu)|en|Nara Line (Kintetsu)|qid=Q692386|lt=Kintetsu Nara Line|1=Nara Line (Kintetsu)}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Pages == * {{ill|Tsujiura senbei|en|Tsujiura senbei|qid=Q7850031|lt=Tsuji-uranai|1=Tsujiura senbei}}
== External Links == * [https://m.facebook.com/hyotanyama.inari/?locale2=ja_JP Hyoutan-yama Inari Shrine - Facebook] * [https://www.city.higashiosaka.lg.jp/0000007193.html Hyoutan-yama Inari Shrine | Higashiosaka City]
{{translated page|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|version=98196867|comment=Imported full ja history}}
